topic: Someone finds an unsigned birthday card in their mailbox
raw_output:
[story 1]

The mail slot rattled, a familiar, unwelcome sound. I pulled the thick envelope out, my fingers brushing against the unfamiliar texture of the paper. It was a card, folded neatly, bearing no address, just a single, elegant script wishing ‘Happy Birthday’ to an unnamed recipient. A sudden tightness gripped my chest, a strange, hot flush rising up my neck. Who was this person? And why was it addressed to someone else? I quickly slid it under the pile of bills, my gaze darting around the quiet living room as if expecting someone to materialize and scrutinize my clumsy discovery. The silence amplified the frantic drumming in my ears.

[story 2]

I picked up the card, the paper surprisingly smooth beneath my fingertips. The ink was a deep, celebratory blue. A wave of awkwardness washed over me, making my palms immediately slick. I stared at the message, the cheerful sentiment feeling utterly misplaced. It was clearly meant for someone else, yet here it was, deposited in my box. I folded it once, then unfolded it again, my thumb tracing the edges repeatedly. I tucked it deep into the pocket of my jeans, hunching my shoulders slightly, wishing the floor would simply swallow me whole. Every glance toward the front door felt like an invitation for judgment.

[story 3]

The mail was always a source of minor anxiety for me; it was too much unsolicited attention. Today, however, the contents of the box stopped me cold. An unsigned birthday card. My heart gave a strange, uneven thump against my ribs. I held it up to the light, examining the simple, bright lettering. A deep sense of exposure settled over me, making my throat feel suddenly dry. I wanted to shove it back into the slot immediately, to erase the anomaly. But my hand remained frozen, hovering over the paper as I wrestled with the urge to either discard it or somehow claim ownership of this anonymous gesture.
